title: DC Is Shooting Off 851,000 Fireworks This July 4
script: 851,000 fireworks? Washington, DC is about to fire off the largest fireworks display in US history — 851,000 shells over 40 minutes along the National Mall, the Potomac River, and eight floating barges, all timed to break the Guinness World Record for the most fireworks launched in a single show. The record is currently held by a church in the Philippines, which set off 810,904 fireworks in 2016. DC is going to top that for America's 250th birthday. Pyrotecnico has 60 technicians on eight Potomac barges plus every site from the Lincoln Memorial to West Potomac Park. The display runs double the usual time. They're doing this on July 4, five days from now. So can 60 people actually pull off the world's biggest fireworks show at once?
